tam.subtitle: Full SG events + MICE envelope at 2025 scale, in SGD.

stacks.name: SG events industry
stacks.source: Deep Market Insights / Apollo SG events 2025: USD 5.46B × 1.34 = SGD 7.32B

stacks.name: SG MICE
stacks.source: Coherent Market Insights SG MICE 2025: USD 4.80B × 1.34 = SGD 6.43B

stacks.name: Overlap adjustment
stacks.source: Triangulation: MICE is a sub-set of broader events; estimate 35% overlap on STB visitor counts.

sam.subtitle: HR engagement-events pool flashcart can plausibly serve.
sam.total_equation: 4,500 SG MNCs/large SMEs × S$78k avg engagement spend
filters: Annual engagement spend (D&D + family day + town hall + perks days)
Excludes wedding, public exhibitions, trade-show floor

stacks.name: Mid-band of HR engagement-events pool
stacks.source: Triangulated from BusinessGo HSBC + Get Out! Events guides; ACRA-derived MNC/large-SME count of 3,000–6,000

som.subtitle: 5-year capture realistic under Elitez Group HR-channel distribution moat.

stacks.name: Elitez-channel-led capture
stacks.source: Internal capacity model: hundreds of pre-existing HR relationships across Elitez Pulse + EOR + Security + Aviation client base; 5% conservative / 7% target / 10% stretch

implications.headline: Wedge SAM, not envelope TAM, drives the plan
implications.body: The S$11.4B SG events envelope is decoy size; flashcart's real ceiling is the S$350M HR engagement-events pool inside it. Year-1 sales targeting must hit named MNC HR Engagement Leads, not the broader MICE category.

implications.headline: S$25M SOM justifies Elitez bolt-on, not standalone Series A
implications.body: 5-yr SOM of S$25M is meaningful as a margin-bearing extension of Elitez Group's HR-relationship moat but is sub-scale for standalone fundraising. Treat flashcart as cross-sell on existing Elitez accounts before solo go-to-market.

implications.headline: MNC vs SME split governs ARPU model
implications.body: MNC penetration of structured engagement events is much higher than SME (see adoption_patterns), so 70%+ of Year-1 capture should come from MNCs at SGD 12k+/yr engagement-partner contracts; SME activations are volume-seeding not revenue-load-bearing.

implications.headline: Hybrid-work shift is structural tailwind
implications.body: 80% of SG employers are hybrid; HR is shifting budget from 1–2 mega-events to a cadence of smaller activations (Get Out! 2026 trends report). More activation slots = more flashcart events; this multiplies SOM if ARPU is held flat.

implications.headline: SG→SEA expansion gated by country-readiness
implications.body: Country readiness shows MY+ID+TH+VN at 2–3 on price tolerance for premium HR tooling. Year 2–3 MY/MNC-ID-Vietnam expansion should be Elitez-cross-sell-only, not direct-sales investments; unit economics outside SG remain unproven.

market_size.methodology_appendix: TAM combines SG Events Industry (USD 5.46B 2025 per Deep Market Insights / Apollo, projected USD 14.18B by 2034 at 11.12% CAGR) and SG MICE (USD 4.8B 2025 per Coherent Market Insights, USD 8.89B by 2032 at 9.20% CAGR), converted to SGD at mid-2025 rate USD/SGD 1.34, with a 35% overlap adjustment because MICE is partly counted inside the broader SG events envelope (especially around STB visitor data: 1.1M MICE arrivals in 2024, 560k in H1 2025, surpassing the 2019 730k baseline). SAM narrows to the HR engagement-events sub-segment flashcart actually plays in (D&Ds, family days, town halls, wellness fairs, perks days). Per-head budget bands from Get Out! Events 2026 SG cost guides: D&D total SGD 100–180/head all-in; family day SGD 30–80/head; town hall SGD 50–150/head; lucky draw prize pool SGD 500–2,000 per event; goodie/door gift SGD 3–10 basic, 25–80 premium, 30–100+ full kits. ACRA active-entity universe is approximately 300,000 SG entities. Triangulating ~3,000–6,000 MNCs / large SMEs running annual D&Ds + a town hall + a family day at SGD 50–150k engagement spend per company per year yields a SAM band of SGD 200–500M; we anchor at the S$350M midpoint pending primary research. SOM is 7% of SAM cumulative over 5 years (range 5–10%), reflecting Elitez Group's structural distribution moat — hundreds of pre-existing HR/payroll relationships routed through Elitez Pulse, EOR, Security, and Aviation client bases. The 7% figure is higher than typical productivity-tool entrants (usually 3–5%) because flashcart enters with warm HR relationships rather than cold-outbound. Honest unknowns flagged for primary research: precise SG engagement-events SGD pool size; whether SG building managers (CapitaLand, Mapletree, Frasers tenant-engagement teams) move into onsite-commerce as a tenant amenity; exact pay-to-play booth pricing at SG MNC offices.

sources.title: Deep Market Insights / Apollo — SG Events Industry
Get Out! Events D&D / family-day / corporate-event 2026 cost guides
Get Out! Events 2026 corporate entertainment trends

policies.title: HPB Workplace Outreach Wellness sunset
policies.body: HPB's Workplace Outreach Wellness programme — which subsidised onsite wellness activities and screenings at SG workplaces — sunsets on 28 February 2026. From March 2026 employers must self-fund or migrate to the Workplace Health Grant. This shifts onsite wellness-fair economics: HR teams that relied on HPB-subsidised vendors must now find new funding routes.
policies.implication_for_us: Brand-funded flashcart pop-ups become more attractive vs HPB-subsidised vendors that just lost their subsidy; lead with 'free to host, brand-funded' positioning.

policies.title: HPB Workplace Health Grant (WHG)
policies.body: Up to S$15,000 cap with 50% co-funding for SG SMEs to run workplace wellness activities. Eligible activities include onsite health screening, mental wellness programmes, and structured wellness fairs. Programme is the primary surviving HPB lever after the Workplace Outreach Wellness sunset.
policies.implication_for_us: Position the wellness-fair flashcart tier as WHG-claimable; SME HR can offset 50% of the activation up to S$15k cap.

policies.title: MOM Workforce Singapore HR initiatives
policies.body: WSG continues to fund HR transformation under WDG(JR+) and Career Conversion Programmes. Engagement and culture-building are explicitly within scope when bundled with HR consulting outcomes — not always direct subsidy but frames the HR buyer's broader budget envelope.
policies.implication_for_us: Where flashcart bundles with HR-consulting outcome (e.g. retention uplift), buyer can route through WSG-funded transformation budget rather than line-item engagement spend.

policies.title: IRAS GST treatment of corporate gifts
policies.body: Input tax claims are allowed on corporate gifts subject to deemed-supply rules above S$200/person/year; below that, no deemed output tax. Practical effect: HR can fully reclaim GST on most goodie-bag and lucky-draw items if maintained under the threshold per person.
policies.implication_for_us: Anchor onsite SKU bands at sub-S$200/employee/event so GST input claims remain clean; this is a buyer-friendly framing in the proposal deck.

policies.title: MAS PayNow corporate adoption
policies.body: PayNow Corporate is now mandatory acceptance for SG corporate banking customers (DBS, OCBC, UOB and major foreign banks). QR-code present-mode payments are the default settlement rail for SG retail and SME merchants, making mobile-checkout flashcart pop-ups capital-light versus card-terminal incumbents.
policies.implication_for_us: Standardise on PayNow QR for every flashcart event; cite 'no card-terminal needed' as a hard cost differentiator vs Stripe-terminal pop-up vendors.

cultural_signals.observation: SG hybrid work has moved engagement budget from 1–2 mega-events/yr to a cadence of smaller, more frequent activations.
cultural_signals.evidence: Get Out! Events 2026 corporate entertainment trends report; HRM Asia 2025 hybrid-work survey shows 80% of SG employers run 3+ days in office.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Flashcart's tailwind: more activation slots per company per year. Lead sales with quarterly-cadence pricing rather than once-a-year contracts.

cultural_signals.observation: Post-COVID, SG HR teams are judged on attendance and post-event NPS, not on vendor revenue.
cultural_signals.evidence: BusinessGo HSBC 2024–25 SG events guide; informal HR Lead conversations across Elitez client base.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Pitch flashcart on attendance + employee-NPS uplift; never pitch as a revenue-share that the HR Lead is P&L-accountable for.

cultural_signals.observation: MNC HR teams default to courier-bag + voucher gifting (Giftano/HoneySpree) when they want 'give the team something' — the reflex is delivery, not onsite.
cultural_signals.evidence: Giftano scaled to 1,000+ partner brands as the SG market leader; HoneySpree active in sub-SGD 10 segment.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Direct comparison must show engagement uplift of an onsite event vs a delivered hamper of the same SGD value; the 'hamper got dropped at desk' mental model is the rival.

economic_signals.implication_for_us: MICE recovery is full — venue and AV vendors are at capacity, increasing pricing pressure for MNC HR teams looking for cheaper slots; flashcart-as-pop-up is structurally lighter than booking ballroom space.

economic_signals.value: S$50–150k/co./yr (D&D + town hall + family day combined)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Flashcart is selling into the lower mid-band of this envelope; the average S$78k mid-point is the right anchor for annual engagement-partner contracts.

economic_signals.value: S$3–10 basic; S$25–80 premium; S$30–100+ full kits
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Anchor flashcart's 'spend redirection' pitch on this band: at 200 ppl × S$5/head saved vs goodie bag = S$1,000 redirect, demonstrably visible to the HR buyer.

adoption_patterns.note: Onsite event-commerce (PayNow QR + curated SKU mobile checkout at corporate events) is sub-10% penetration in SG SMEs and ~22% in MNCs as a rough triangulation — most current MNC events have a pop-up of some kind (florist, photo booth, sample stall) but very few have curated multi-SKU mobile checkout. Honest caveat: no public penetration figure exists; this is internal triangulation against ACRA + Elitez client visibility, and primary research would be needed to harden the number.

trends.title: Activation cadence shift: fewer mega-events, more frequent micro-activations
trends.evidence: Get Out! Events 2026 trends report calls out the shift from one annual D&D to quarterly + ad-hoc activations as the dominant SG mid-market pattern post-hybrid.
trends.implication_for_us: Build pricing around quarterly activation packages, not one-off contracts; this multiplies SOM if flashcart can hold ARPU per slot.

trends.title: Brand-funded surplus inventory rail in SG concentrated in B2C members club (OnTheList) — unbridged to HR channel
trends.evidence: OnTheList SG runs monthly pop-ups to 62k B2C members; no SG player aggregates clearance and routes through HR-channel distribution at corporate-event venues.
trends.implication_for_us: This is the genuine whitespace: the bridge from supplier-funded surplus to HR-channel distribution at events. Defending it depends on being first.

trends.title: PayNow QR + mobile checkout is SG default; pop-up vendors still default to Stripe terminal or cash
trends.evidence: MAS PayNow Corporate adoption is universal across major SG banks since 2023; HitPay PayNow guide shows QR present-mode is the standard SG retail rail.
trends.implication_for_us: Operational distinctiveness: PayNow QR + curated SKU mobile checkout flow is capital-light vs card-terminal incumbents. This is a moat for the operations layer.

trends.title: Live commerce / livestream-shopping pattern (Whatnot USD 6B+ GMV 2025) validates flash + commerce thesis
trends.evidence: ecommercefastlane.com 2026 round-up: Whatnot, TalkShopLive, Bambuser, NTWRK demonstrate viability of curated time-boxed flash retail in non-traditional channels.
trends.implication_for_us: International precedent strengthens the thesis even though the channel (HR-distributed onsite vs B2C livestream) is different; useful for investor and MNC-HR storytelling.

trends.title: SG building managers (CapitaLand, Mapletree, Frasers) explore tenant-engagement amenities
trends.evidence: No public signal yet of REIT tenant-engagement teams operating onsite commerce; this trend is speculative and listed as a primary-research unknown.
trends.implication_for_us: Watch CapitaLand / Mapletree tenant-engagement RFPs; pre-empt by partnering on building-lobby pop-up slots before they build in-house.
